title: 3/28/18, 3 Roller Rides: 66.16Mi 4:32. History Test but didn't save

enrichment.summary: Kyle recaps his day in Boulder: he slept in after feeling tired, ran errands, did three roller rides totaling 66 miles, and recorded a weekly history test video that failed to save. He reflects on how being high hurt his history test performance and outlines how he plans to do it properly next time, including using a random number generator on camera to prove legitimacy.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: You have to study right before the test so the decades are in short-term memory
Getting your ass kicked on camera is valuable because it encourages others to participate
Must use a video camera and random number generator to prove the decade selection is not rigged
31 degrees is too cold; he won't ride below that as a beginner rule for himself
Being high hurt his memory and history recall during the test

claims.subject: Colonialism hat
claims.claim: The Colonialism hat is from a show where people in Rome who go and live in the colonies wear these hats.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Last night, Kyle felt really tired and distracted because he had some of the old stuff he loves.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle slept in today but it wasn't that late.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle went to the store and got a bunch of power bars for a long ride.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle made a bunch of key copies because he needed more keys.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle rode for 40 minutes but got exhausted, dropping down to 110 watts and barely able to focus.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle took a nap after riding and then worked for two hours, averaging 150 watts and 17 miles an hour.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle did a weekly history test video today but had issues saving it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle was high while doing the history test video and kept forgetting what decades he was covering.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle didn't study for the history test, which made his memory of the history not good.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to study for the next history test by scanning the timeline and taking the test right after to improve performance.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes getting your ass kicked is valuable because it encourages people to fail and learn.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle thinks making yourself look stupid can be a way to get others on board, but it didn't work for him.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to use a video camera for the next history test to prove he's not making up the combo to look smart.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle checks the weather and decides it's too cold to ride tomorrow because he's just starting out and needs to be easy on himself.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle's rule is to ride for one or two hours, no more.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to ride now because he had a nap and can sleep in the morning.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is going to listen to music and smoke to distract himself from his thoughts.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle mentions he did two rides but might load the next one tomorrow if he hasn't already.
